Unknown Primary - Cervical Nodes.rules: There should be histological confirmation of squamous cell carcinoma with lymph node metastases but without an identified primary carcinoma. Histological methods should be used to identify EBV and HPV/p16-associated tumours. If there is evidence of EBV, the nasopharyngeal classification is applied. If there is evidence of HPV and/or positive immunohistochemistry p16 overexpression, the p16-positive oropharyngeal classification is applied. Oesophagus.rules: The classification applies only to carcinomas and includes adenocarcinomas of the oesophagogastric/gastroesophageal junction (excluding carcinomas of minor salivary glands of the upper third of the oesophagus). There should be histological confirmation of the disease and division of cases by topographic localisation and histological type. A tumour the epicentre of which is within 2 cm of the oesophagogastric junction and also extends into the oesophagus is classified and staged using the oesophageal scheme. Cancers involving the oesophagogastric junction (OGJ) whose epicentre is within the proximal 2 cm of the cardia (Siewert types I/II) are to be staged as oesophageal cancers. Anatomical Subsites 1. Cervical oesophagus (C15.0): This commences at the lower border of the cricoid cartilage and ends at the thoracic inlet (suprasternal notch), approximately 18 cm from the upper incisor teeth. 2. Intrathoracic oesophagus: a) The upper thoracic portion (C15.3) extending from the thoracic inlet to the level of the tracheal bifurcation, approximately 24 cm from the upper incisor teeth. b) The mid-thoracic portion (C15.4) is the proximal half of the oesophagus between the tracheal bifurcation and the oesophagogastric junction. The lower level is approximately 32 cm from the upper incisor teeth. c) The lower thoracic portion (C15.5), approximately 8 cm in length (includes abdominal oesophagus), is the distal half of the oesophagus between the tra- cheal bifurcation and the oesophagogastric junction. The lower level is approx- imately 40 cm from the upper incisor teeth. 3. Oesophagogastric junction (C16.7): Cancers involving the oesophagogastric junc- tion (OGJ) whose epicentre is within the proximal 2 cm of the cardia (Siewert types I/II) are to be staged as oesophageal cancers. Cancers whose epicentre is more than 2 cm distal from the OGJ will be staged using the stomach cancer TNM and stage even if the OGJ is involved.

Soft Tissues.rules: The classification only applies to malignant and uncertain behaviour mesenchymal tumours with metastatic potential (including sarcomas). Histological Types of Tumour The following histological types are not included: ▪▪ Kaposi sarcoma ▪▪ Dermatofibrosarcoma (protuberans) ▪▪ Fibromatosis (desmoid tumour) ▪▪ Sarcoma arising from the dura mater and brain ▪▪ Angiosarcoma, an aggressive sarcoma, is excluded because its natural history is not consistent with the classification. For tumours in paediatric patients, please see page 242. Note Malignant phyllodes tumour is staged as a soft tissue sarcoma of the superficial trunk. Regional Lymph Nodes The regional lymph nodes are those appropriate to the site of the primary tumour. Regional node involvement is rare, and cases in which nodal status is not assessed either clinically or pathologically could be considered N0 instead of cNX or pNX.

A tumour involving the vulva is classified as carcinoma of the vulva. There should be histological confirmation of the disease. pT.T0: No evidence of primary tumour (e.g., histological scar in testis)
pT.Tis: Intratubular germ cell neoplasia (carcinoma in situ)
pT.T1: Tumour limited to testis, including the rete testis, but without vascular/lym- phatic invasion or invasion of the epididymis; tumour may invade tunica albuginea but not tunica vaginalis1
pT.T2: Tumour limited to testis with vascular/lymphatic invasion, or invading hilar soft tissue or epididymis or tumour extending through tunica albuginea with involvement of tunica vaginalis
pT.T3: Tumour invades spermatic cord with or without vascular/lymphatic invasion2
pT.T4: Tumour invades scrotum with or without vascular/lymphatic invasion

T.T1: Tumour 7 cm or less in greatest dimension, limited to the kidney
T.T1a: Tumour 4 cm or less
T.T1b: Tumour more than 4 cm but not more than 7 cm
T.T2: Tumour more than 7 cm in greatest dimension, limited to the kidney
T.T2a: Tumour more than 7 cm but not more than 10 cm
T.T2b: Tumour more than 10 cm, limited to the kidney
T.T3: Tumour extends into major veins or perinephric tissues but not into the ipsi- lateral adrenal gland and not beyond Gerota fascia
T.T3a: Tumour extends into the renal vein or its segmental branches, or tumour invades the pelvicalyceal system or tumour invades perirenal and/or renal sinus fat (peripelvic) fat but not beyond Gerota fascia
T.T3b: Tumour extends into vena cava below diaphragm
T.T3c: Tumour extends into vena cava above the diaphragm or invades the wall of the vena cava
T.T4: Tumour invades beyond Gerota fascia (including contiguous extension into the ipsilateral adrenal gland)

N.NX: Regional lymph nodes cannot be assessed
N.N0: No regional lymph node metastasis Urological

T.T1: Tumour invades subepithelial connective tissue
T.T2: Tumour invades muscularis
T.T3: (Renal pelvis) Tumour invades beyond muscularis into peripelvic fat or renal parenchyma (Ureter) Tumour invades beyond muscularis into periureteric fat
T.T4: Tumour invades adjacent organs or through the kidney into perinephric fat

N.NX: Regional lymph nodes cannot be assessed
N.N1: Metastasis in a single lymph node 2 cm or less in greatest dimension
N.N2: Metastasis in a single lymph node more than 2 cm or multiple lymph nodes

T.T1: Tumour invades subepithelial connective tissue
T.T2: Tumour invades muscularis propria
T.T2a: Tumour invades superficial muscularis propria (inner half)
T.T2b: Tumour invades deep muscularis propria (outer half)
T.T3: Tumour invades perivesical tissue:
T.T4: Tumour invades any of the following: prostate stroma, seminal vesicles, uterus, vagina, pelvic wall or abdominal wall
T.T4a: Tumour invades prostate stroma, seminal vesicles, uterus or vagina
T.T4b: Tumour invades pelvic wall or abdominal wall

N.NX: Regional lymph nodes cannot be assessed
N.N0: No regional lymph node metastasis Urological
N.N1: Metastasis in a single lymph node in the true pelvis (hypogastric, obturator, external iliac or presacral)
N.N2: Metastasis in multiple regional lymph nodes in the true pelvis (hypogastric, obturator, external iliac or presacral)
N.N3: Metastasis in a common iliac lymph node(s)

Urethra.rules: The classification applies to carcinomas of the urethra (ICD-O-4 C68.0) and urothe- lial carcinomas of the prostate (ICD-O-4 C61.9) and prostatic urethra. There should be histological or cytological confirmation of the disease.

T.T0: No evidence of primary tumour Urethra (male and female)
T.Ta: Non-invasive papillary, polypoid or verrucous carcinoma
T.Tis: Carcinoma in situ, involving the prostatic urethra, periurethral or prostatic ducts without stromal invasion
T.T1: Tumour invades subepithelial connective tissue (for tumours involving prostatic urethra only)
T.T2: Tumour invades any of the following: prostatic stroma, corpus spongiosum or periurethral muscle
T.T3: Tumour invades any of the following: corpus cavernosum, beyond prostatic capsule or bladder neck (extraprostatic extension)
T.T4: Tumour invades other adjacent organs (invasion of the bladder or rectum)

N.NX: Regional lymph nodes cannot be assessed
N.N1: Metastasis in a single lymph node
N.N2: Metastasis in multiple lymph nodes

Adrenal Cortex.rules: This classification applies only to carcinomas of the adrenal cortex. It does not apply Adrenal Cortex to tumours of the adrenal medulla or sarcomas.

T.T1: Tumour 5 cm or less in greatest dimension, no extra-adrenal invasion
T.T2: Tumour greater than 5 cm, no extra-adrenal invasion
T.T3: Tumour of any size with local invasion, but not invading adjacent organs*
T.T4: Tumour of any size with invasion of adjacent organs*

N.NX: Regional lymph nodes cannot be assessed
N.N1: Metastasis in regional lymph node(s) TNM Classification of Malignant Tumours, Ninth Edition. Edited by James Brierley, Meredith Giuliani, Brian O’Sullivan, Brian Rous, and Elizabeth Van Eycken.

Adrenal Medulla and Extra-Adrenal Paraga.tumour: Adrenal Medulla and Extra-Adrenal Paraganglia Tumours
Adrenal Medulla and Extra-Adrenal Paraga.rules: This classification applies to paraganglion neuroendocrine tumours, consisting of phaeochromocytomas of the adrenal medulla (C74.1) and paragangliomasa of the aortic body and other paragangliomas (C75.5). Carotid body tumours are excluded.b Notes a Sympathetic paragangliomas are functional. b Parasympathetic paragangliomas are non-functional and are commonly found in the head and neck region (i.e., carotid body tumours) and usually have an excellent prognosis. The definitions of the T, N and M categories correspond with the AJCC 8th edition/version. Adrenal Medulla

T.T1: Phaeochromocytoma 5 cm or less in greatest dimension, no extra- adrenal invasion
T.T2: Phaeochromocytoma greater than 5 cm in greatest dimension, no extra-adrenal invasion Paragangliomaa of any size, no local invasion
T.T3: Tumour of any size with local invasion, into adjoining tissues or adjacent organsa

N.NX: Regional lymph nodes cannot be assessed

T.T1: Tumour 5 mm or less in greatest dimension invades through the conjunctival basement membrane
T.T2: Tumour more than 5 mm in greatest dimension, invades through the conjunctival basement membrane without invasion of adjacent structures*
T.T3: Tumour invades adjacent structures*
T.T4: Tumour invades the orbit or beyond
T.T4a: Tumour invades orbital soft tissues, without bone invasion
T.T4b: Tumour invades bone

N.NX: Regional lymph nodes cannot be assessed

T.Tis: Melanoma confined to the conjunctival epithelium (in situ)a
T.T1: Melanoma of the bulbar conjunctiva
T.T1a: Tumour involves less than or equal to one quadrantb
T.T1b: Tumour involves more than one but less than or equal to two quadrants
T.T1c: Tumour involves more than two but less than or equal to three quadrants
T.T1d: Tumour involves more than three quadrants
T.T2: Melanoma of the non-bulbar conjunctiva involving palpebral, forniceal and/or caruncular conjunctiva
T.T2a: Non-caruncular tumour involves less than or equal to one quadrant
T.T2b: Non-caruncular tumour involves more than one quadrant
T.T2c: Caruncular tumour involves less than or equal to one quadrant of conjunctiva
T.T2d: Caruncular tumour involves more than one quadrant of conjunctiva
T.T3: Tumour with local invasion into:
T.T3d: Paranasal sinus, nasolacrimal duct and/or lacrimal gland
T.T4: Tumour invades central nervous system

N.NX: Regional lymph nodes cannot be assessed

Melanoma of Uvea.rules: There should be histological confirmation of the disease. The following are the procedures for assessing T, N and M categories.

T.T0: No evidence of primary tumour Ophthalmic Iris*
T.T1: Tumour limited to iris
T.T1a: not more than 3 clock hours in size
T.T1b: more than 3 clock hours in size
T.T2: Tumour confluent with or extending into the ciliary body, choroid or both
T.T2a: Tumour confluent with or extending into the ciliary body without secondary glaucoma
T.T2b: Tumour confluent with or extending into the choroid without secondary glaucoma
T.T2c: Tumour confluent with or extending into the ciliary body and/or choroid with secondary glaucoma
T.T3: Tumour confluent with or extending into the ciliary body, choroid or both, with scleral extension
T.T4: Tumour with extrascleral extension
T.T4a: less than or equal to 5 mm in diameter
T.T4b: more than 5 mm in diameter

N.NX: Regional lymph nodes cannot be assessed

M.M1a: Largest metastasis 3 cm or less in greatest dimension
M.M1b: Largest metastasis is larger than 3 cm in greatest dimension but not larger than 8 cm
M.M1c: Largest metastasis is larger than 8 cm in greatest dimension

Retinoblastoma.rules: In bilateral cases, the eyes should be classified separately. The classification does not apply to complete spontaneous regression of the tumour. There should be histological confirmation of the disease in an enucleated eye. For Retinoblastoma in paediatric patients, please also see page 247.

T.T1: Tumour confined to the retina with subretinal fluid not more than 5 mm from the base of any tumour, without retinal detachment
T.T1a: No tumour in either eye is greater than 3 mm in largest dimension or located closer than 1.5 mm to the optic nerve or fovea
T.T1b: At least one tumour is greater than 3 mm in largest dimension or located closer than 1.5 mm to the optic nerve or fovea. No retinal detachment or subretinal fluid beyond 5 mm from the base of the tumour
T.T2: Tumours with vitreous or subretinal seeding or retinal detachment
T.T2a: Tumour with subretinal fluid more than 5 mm from the base of any tumour
T.T2b: Tumour with vitreous and/or subretinal seeding
T.T3: Severe intraocular disease
T.T3b: Invasion of choroid, pars plana, ciliary body, lens, zonules, iris or anterior chamber
T.T3c: Raised intraocular pressure with neovascularisation and/or buphthalmos
T.T3d: Hyphema and/or massive vitreous haemorrhage cT3e Aseptic orbital cellulitis
T.T4: Extraocular tumour
T.T4a: Invasion of optic nerve or orbital tissues
T.T4b: Extraocular invasion with proptosis and/or orbital mass

N.NX: Regional lymph nodes cannot be assessed

M.M1a: Single or multiple metastasis to sites other than CNS or brain
M.M1b: Metastasis to the CNS including brain TNM Pathological Classification pTX Primary tumour cannot be assessed pT0 No evidence of primary tumour pT1 Tumour confined to eye with no optic nerve or choroidal invasion pT2 Tumour with intraocular invasion pT2a Focal choroidal invasion and pre-or intralaminar invasion of the optic nerve head pT2b Tumour invasion of stroma of iris and/or trabecular meshwork and/or Schlemm canal pT3 Tumour with significant local invasion pT3a Choroidal invasion larger than 3 mm in diameter or multiple foci of invasion totalling more than 3 mm or any full-thickness involvement pT3b Retrolaminar invasion of optic nerve without invasion of transected end of optic nerve pT3c Partial-thickness involvement of sclera within the inner two-thirds pT3d Full-thickness invasion into outer third of the sclera and/or invasion into or around emissary channels pT4 Extraocular extension: Tumour invades optic nerve at transected end, in menin- geal space around the optic nerve, full-thickness invasion of the sclera with invasion of the episclera, adipose tissue, extraocular muscle, bone, conjunctiva or eyelid. pNX Regional lymph nodes cannot be assessed pN0 No regional lymph node involvement pN1 Regional lymph node involvement

Sarcoma of Orbit.rules: The classification applies to sarcomas of soft tissue and bone. There should be histological confirmation of the disease and division of cases by histological type.

T.T1: Tumour 20 mm or less in greatest dimension
T.T2: Tumour more than 20 mm in greatest dimension without invasion of globe or bony wall
T.T3: Tumour of any size with invasion of orbital tissues and/or bony walls
T.T4: Tumour invades globe or periorbital structure, such as eyelids, temporal fossa, nasal cavity and paranasal sinuses, and/or central nervous system Ophthalmic

N.NX: Regional lymph nodes cannot be assessed

Carcinoma of Lacrimal Gland.rules: There should be histological confirmation of the disease and division of cases by histological type.

T.T1: Tumour 2 cm or less in greatest dimension, with or without extraglandular extension into the orbital soft tissue
T.T1a: No periosteal or bone involvement
T.T1b: Periosteal involvement without bone involvement
T.T2: Tumour more than 2 cm but not more than 4 cm in greatest dimension.
T.T3: Tumour more than 4 cm or with extraglandular extension into orbital soft Ophthalmic tissue, including optic nerve, or globe
T.T4: Tumour invades adjacent structures (sinuses, temporal fossa, pterygoid fossa, superior orbital fissure, cavernous sinus and/or brain)
T.T4a: Not more than 2 cm in greatest dimension
T.T4b: More than 2 cm but not more than 4 cm in greatest dimension
T.T4c: More than 4 cm in greatest dimension

N.NX: Regional lymph nodes cannot be assessed

Brain & Spinal Cord.rules: The classification applies only to medulloblastomas, medulloepithelioma, CNS embry- onal tumour, atypical teratoid/rhabdoid tumour and pineoblastoma. There should be histological confirmation of the disease and division of cases by histological type. For paediatric patients, please see page 249.

M.M1b: M2 Microscopic confirmation intracranial spread beyond primary site
M.M1c: M3 Microscopic confirmation of gross spinal subarachnoid seeding
M.M1a: M1 Microscopic confirmation of tumour cells present in cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) by cytology

tnm.T_note: No T classification applies; grading-based system

Hodgkin Lymphoma.classification_note: Uses Ann Arbor/Lugano staging system, not TNM

Non-Hodgkin Lymphomas.rules: The classification applies to primary cutaneous lymphoma, mycosis fungoides (MF), Sézary syndrome (SS) and T and B Cell Cutaneous Lymphoma. There should be histological confirmation of the disease. Anatomical Sites The following sites are identified by the ICD-O topography rubrics: 1. External ear and other parts of face (excluding lip and eyelid) (C44. 2-3) 2. Scalp and neck (C44.4) 3. Trunk (including anal margin and perianal skin) (C44.5, C21.3) 4. Arm and shoulder (C44.6) 5. Leg and hip (C44.7) 6. Labium majus and minus, clitoris and vulva (C51. 0-2, 8-9) 7. Prepuce, glans penis, body of penis and penis (C60. 0-2, 8-9) 8. Scrotum (C63.2)

T.T1: Solitary lesion
T.T1a: <5 cm in diameter
T.T1b: ≥5 cm in diameter
T.T2: Multiple lesions limited to one body region or two contiguous body regions
T.T2b: All disease ≥15 cm to <30 cm in diameter
T.T2c: All disease ≥30 cm in diameter without extending beyond 2 contiguous body regions
T.T3: Generalised disease
T.T3a: Multiple lesions involving two non-contiguous body lesions
T.T3b: Three or more body regions involved

N.N0: No clinically abnormal peripheral lymph nodes
N.N1: Involvement of 1 peripheral nodal region that drains the area of involved skin
N.N2: Involvement of > 2 peripheral node regions or involvement of any node region that does not drain the area of involved skin
N.N3: Involvement of central node on biopsy

M.M1: M1 Visceral involvement Stage There is no stage. Mycosis Fungoides or Sézary syndrome
